content:
# 🚨 MANDATORY: Team Memory System 🚨

A shared team memory stores critical gotchas, conventions, and debugging insights across sessions.

## REQUIRED: /recall BEFORE Starting Any Task

You MUST run /recall at the start of every task to check for relevant prior knowledge. Skipping this risks repeating known mistakes and wasting time on already-solved problems.

```
/recall "<topic or keyword relevant to your task>"
```

Examples:
- Working on Stripe integration? → /recall "Stripe"
- Fixing a database query? → /recall "database" or /recall "Drizzle"
- Debugging a test? → /recall "testing patterns"
- Working on team creation? → /recall "team" or /recall "createId"
- Touching waivers? → /recall "waiver"

**Do NOT skip this step.** Past sessions have stored critical gotchas that will save significant debugging time.

## REQUIRED: /remember After Discoveries

When you discover something useful — a gotcha, root cause, convention, or debugging insight — you MUST save it immediately with /remember. Do not wait until the end of the session.

```
/remember "<what you learned>" --category=gotcha|convention|debugging|architecture|workflow --priority=critical|moderate|ephemeral
```

## Summary

- **BEFORE work**: /recall to load relevant memories (MANDATORY)
- **DURING work**: /remember as soon as you discover something worth saving (MANDATORY)
- Failure to use team memory means the team loses knowledge and repeats mistakes.

## deepwiki
DeepWiki MCP provides AI-powered documentation for GitHub repositories.

Available tools:
- read_wiki_structure: Get a list of documentation topics for a repository
- read_wiki_contents: View full documentation about a repository
- ask_question: Ask any question about a repository and get an AI-powered answer
- list_available_repos: List your available repositories (private mode only)
- generate_wiki: Generate a codebase wiki for a repository — only use when explicitly requested by the user (private mode only)
- devin_knowledge_manage: Manage Devin knowledge notes and suggestions — list, search, get, create, update, delete notes, view folder structure, list/view/dismiss knowledge suggestions (private mode only)
- devin_playbook_manage: Manage Devin playbooks — list, get, create, update, delete (private mode only)
- devin_schedule_manage: Manage scheduled Devin sessions — list, get, create, update, delete (private mode only)
- devin_session_create: Create one or more child Devin sessions (private mode only)
- devin_session_interact: Manage a Devin session — get status, send messages, sleep/terminate/archive, read messages & attachments, manage tags (private mode only)
- devin_session_events: Inspect session events — list summaries, fetch full details, or search event contents (private mode only)
- devin_session_search: Search and filter Devin sessions (private mode only)
- list_integrations: List all native integrations and MCP servers with their status and settings URLs (private mode only)

## plugin:linear:linear
When passing string values to tools, send the content directly without escape sequences. For example, use real newlines in markdown content rather than literal backslash-n (\n) characters.

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, use Config tool.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (triggers) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/triggers.
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- agent-browser: Browser automation CLI for AI agents. Use when the user needs to interact with websites, including navigating pages, filling forms, clicking buttons, taking screenshots, extracting data, testing web apps, or automating any browser task. Triggers include requests to "open a website", "fill out a form", "click a button", "take a screenshot", "scrape data from a page", "test this web app", "login to a site", "automate browser actions", or any task requiring programmatic web interaction.
- seo-aeo-best-practices: SEO and AEO best practices for metadata, Open Graph, sitemaps, robots.txt, hreflang, JSON-LD structured data, EEAT, and content optimized for search engines and AI answer surfaces. Use this skill when implementing page SEO, technical SEO, schema markup, international SEO, AI-overview readiness, or improving content for Google, ChatGPT, Perplexity, and similar assistants.
- self-improving-agent: A universal self-improving agent that learns from ALL skill experiences. Uses multi-memory architecture (semantic + episodic + working) to continuously evolve the codebase. Auto-triggers on skill completion/error with hooks-based self-correction.
- web-design-guidelines: Review UI code for Web Interface Guidelines compliance. Use when asked to "review my UI", "check accessibility", "audit design", "review UX", or "check my site against best practices".
- linear: Manage issues, projects & team workflows in Linear. Use when the user wants to read, create or updates tickets in Linear.
- breadboarding: Transform a workflow description into affordance tables showing UI and Code affordances with their wiring. Use to map existing systems or design new ones from shaped parts.
- repo-autopsy: Clone a GitHub repo into a local cache and perform deep analysis using full local toolchain (rg, ast-grep, git, fd, tokei, gitleaks). Use when the user wants to inspect, explore, audit, or understand an external GitHub repository — structure, dependencies, hotspots, exports, secrets, code stats, git blame, or targeted file reads. Triggers on phrases like "autopsy", "analyze this repo", "clone and explore", "what's inside owner/repo", or any request to investigate a third-party GitHub project without adding it to the current working tree.
- adr-skill: Create and maintain Architecture Decision Records (ADRs) optimized for agentic coding workflows. Use when you need to propose, write, update, accept/reject, deprecate, or supersede an ADR; bootstrap an adr folder and index; consult existing ADRs before implementing changes; or enforce ADR conventions. This skill uses Socratic questioning to capture intent before drafting, and validates output against an agent-readiness checklist.
- breadboard-reflection: Breadboard Analysis
- stripe-best-practices: Best practices for building Stripe integrations
- frontend-design: Create distinctive, production-grade frontend interfaces with high design quality. Use this skill when the user asks to build web components, pages, artifacts, posters, or applications (examples include websites, landing pages, dashboards, React components, HTML/CSS layouts, or when styling/beautifying any web UI). Generates creative, polished code and UI design that avoids generic AI aesthetics.
- vercel-react-best-practices: React and Next.js performance optimization guidelines from Vercel Engineering. This skill should be used when writing, reviewing, or refactoring React/Next.js code to ensure optimal performance patterns. Triggers on tasks involving React components, Next.js pages, data fetching, bundle optimization, or performance improvements.
- vercel-composition-patterns: React composition patterns that scale. Use when refactoring components with boolean prop proliferation, building flexible component libraries, or designing reusable APIs. Triggers on tasks involving compound components, render props, context providers, or component architecture. Includes React 19 API changes.
- shaping: Use this methodology when collaboratively shaping a solution with the user - iterating on problem definition (requirements) and solution options (shapes).
- tanstack-start-server-only: TanStack Start server-only patterns using createServerOnlyFn and cloudflare:workers env access.
Use when: accessing Cloudflare environment variables (APP_URL, STRIPE_SECRET_KEY, etc.),
creating server-only utility functions, working with Stripe or other server-only APIs,
or when you need to ensure code only runs on the server and throws if called from client.
Covers createServerOnlyFn vs createServerFn differences, env access patterns, and centralized
server-only utilities.
- documentation: Centralizes Diataxis documentation framework guidance so Claude can write, review, or improve documentation following the four distinct types (tutorials, how-to guides, reference, explanation).
- athlete-docs: Documentation guidance for competition athletes and volunteers in WODsmith. Use when writing, reviewing, or improving athlete-facing documentation including registration, scheduling, workout viewing, leaderboards, check-in, and volunteer coordination.
- ralph-mode: Autonomous AI coding loop that ships features while you sleep. Use when the user wants to run Ralph, start an autonomous coding session, work overnight, or let Claude implement multiple user stories autonomously. Triggers on "ralph", "autonomous mode", "overnight coding", "autonomous loop", or requests to implement multiple stories without supervision.
- server-fn-auth: Authentication and authorization patterns for TanStack Start server functions.
Use when: adding auth to a new server function, fixing missing authorization,
checking user permissions, protecting endpoints, or implementing organizer/volunteer
access control for competitions.
- test: Primary entry point for testing guidance. Routes to appropriate test type (unit, integration, E2E) based on what you're testing. Use when asked about "test", "testing", "write tests", "TDD", or test strategy. Embeds Testing Trophy philosophy and TDD Red-Green-Refactor as core discipline.
- claim-url-lookup: Look up claim URLs for placeholder users created via manual registration. Use when needing to find the sign-up claim link for a specific email address, debug claim token issues, or verify claim tokens exist in local KV. Triggers on "claim URL", "claim token", "claim link", "placeholder user sign-up link", or requests to find the registration invite link for an email.
- competition-organizer-docs: Generate and review documentation for competition organizers using Diataxis framework. Use when writing docs for organizer workflows like creating competitions, managing divisions, scheduling heats, handling registrations, entering results, or managing volunteers/judges.
- type-safety: Fix type assertions and improve TypeScript type safety. Use when encountering 'as unknown as' casts, manual type definitions that duplicate schema types, or unclear type errors in database queries, especially with Drizzle ORM relations. Also use when verifying types
- date-timezone: Handle date and timezone conversions correctly in forms and displays. Use when working with: Date inputs, new Date(), formatDate, toLocaleDateString, getDate(), getMonth(), getFullYear(), date parsing, UTC conversion, timezone issues, dates displaying wrong day, date-only fields in forms, YYYY-MM-DD parsing. Triggers: dates off by one day, timezone bugs, form date handling, competition dates, event dates, calendar dates.
- integration-test: Guide for writing integration tests with Vitest and Testing Library. Use when testing multi-component workflows, database interactions, React components with context providers, or full user flows. Covers the Testing Trophy philosophy (integration > unit), factory patterns for test data, MSW for network mocking, async testing patterns (waitFor, findBy), and custom render with providers. Use this for tests that cross multiple modules or layers of the application.
- permissions: Guide for working with team-based permissions and authorization in the WODsmith codebase. Use when touching TEAM_PERMISSIONS constants, hasTeamPermission/requireTeamPermission functions, adding permission checks to actions or server functions, creating features requiring authorization, or ensuring client-server permission consistency.
- unit-test: Guide for writing unit tests with Vitest. Use when writing tests for service functions, pure logic, or webhook handlers. Covers TDD Red-Green-Refactor cycle, Arrange-Act-Assert pattern, anti-patterns, pure function testing, and mocking at boundaries.
- tanstack-start-boundaries: TanStack Start server/client code boundary patterns. Use when: debugging virtual module resolution errors (tanstack-start-injected-head-scripts, tanstack-start-manifest, #tanstack-router-entry), importing from @tanstack/react-start/server, using getCookie/getRequestHeaders/getRequestUrl, adding providers to router.tsx, or when Vite fails to resolve TanStack Start imports. Covers createServerFn patterns, dynamic imports, and proper placement of server-only code.
- skill-creator: Guide for creating effective skills using TypeScript/Bun scripts (Windows-compatible). Use when users want to create a new skill (or update an existing skill) that extends Claude's capabilities with specialized knowledge, workflows, or tool integrations.
- drag-and-drop: Implement drag and drop using @atlaskit/pragmatic-drag-and-drop. Use when implementing sortable lists, reorderable items, kanban boards, or any drag-drop interactions. Covers draggable setup, drop targets, edge detection, drag previews, and critical state management patterns to avoid performance issues.
- my-pr-comments: Loads the pr review comments for the current branch.
- review: Centralizes persona-driven code reviews (Carmack, React core, Anders, React Router, Next.js, etc.) so Claude can pick or combine expert viewpoints when the user asks for a code review or perspective-specific critique. Use when user asks for code review, architecture feedback, or expert perspective on implementation.
- local-db: Handle local database schema changes with Drizzle and PlanetScale. Use when making schema changes to src/db/schema.ts, adding/modifying database tables or columns, or when asked about database migrations. Covers the push-based local development workflow and when to generate migrations.
- e2e-test: End-to-end testing with Playwright. Use when writing E2E tests, implementing page objects, setting up test fixtures, handling authentication in tests, adding visual regression testing, or configuring E2E tests for CI. Covers when to use E2E vs integration tests, critical path identification, page object pattern, test isolation, and CI considerations.
- team-memory: Store, search, and manage team memory from AI coding sessions. Use /remember to save observations (conventions, gotchas, debugging tips, architecture decisions, workflows). Use /recall to search memories by semantic query. Use /feedback to rate memory quality (helpful/harmful/irrelevant). Triggers on any request to save, recall, search, or rate team knowledge.
- alchemy-cloudflare: Alchemy IaC patterns for deploying TanStack Start apps to Cloudflare Workers with PlanetScale databases via Hyperdrive. Use when setting up new TanStack Start projects, configuring Alchemy deployments, working with PlanetScale/Drizzle migrations, local development with Cloudflare bindings, or deploying to custom domains.
- logging: Observability logging patterns for server functions in TanStack Start on Cloudflare Workers. Use when: adding logging to server functions, debugging request flows, tracking entity CRUD operations, correlating logs across requests, adding observability to new features, troubleshooting request tracing, working with PostHog/OpenTelemetry logs. Triggers: logInfo, logError, logWarning, withRequestContext, addRequestContextAttribute, logEntityCreated, request tracing, observability, correlation IDs.
- create-pr: Create a PR from current changes with PRD gif
- sync-diagrams: Store and synchronize digrams with codebase in ai/diagrams directory
- linear-project: Work on a Linear project with structured workflow
- test-pr: Review PR changes and provide comprehensive test coverage recommendations using unit, integration, and e2e testing skills
- fix-lint: /fix-lint
- swarm:inbox: Check swarm mail inbox for messages from other agents
- swarm:hive: Query and manage swarm tasks (cells)
- swarm:status: Check swarm coordination status - workers, messages, reservations
- swarm:swarm: Decompose a task into parallel subtasks and coordinate execution
- swarm:handoff: Properly end a swarm session - release reservations, sync state, generate continuation prompt
- swarm:swarm-coordination: Multi-agent coordination patterns for OpenCode swarm workflows. Use when work benefits from parallelization or coordination.
- swarm:always-on-guidance: Always-on rule-oriented guidance for claude-plugin agents. Use to align behavior, tool usage, and model-specific defaults (GPT-5.2-code vs Opus 4.5) while avoiding deprecated bd/cass references.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

Before starting work, run `lat search` with one or more queries describing the user's intent.
ALWAYS do this, even when the task seems straightforward — search results may reveal critical design details, protocols, or constraints.
Use `lat section` to read the full content of relevant matches.
Do not read files, write code, or run commands until you have searched.

Remember: `lat.md/` must stay in sync with the codebase. If you change code, update the relevant sections in `lat.md/` and run `lat check` before finishing.

Get the current pr number:

```bash
gh pr view --json "url" | jq '.url'
```

Now you can run this command to get all the pr comments in JSON

```bash
gh api repos/corpaxe/{reponame}/pulls/{prnumber}/comments --paginate --jq '.[] | {file: .path, line: .line, body: .body, author: .user.login}'
```

```bash
gh pr view --json "url" | jq '.url'

# outputs
"https://github.com/corpaxe/External.Frontend/pull/2972"

gh api   repos/corpaxe/External.Frontend/pulls/2976/comments | jq '.[] | {file: .path, line: .line, body: .body, author: .user.login}'
```

{"author":"coderabbitai[bot]","body":"_⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🔴 Critical_

<details>
<summary>🧩 Analysis chain</summary>

🌐 Web query:

`Do MySQL and PlanetScale UNIQUE indexes allow multiple NULL values in indexed columns, and do duplicate-key updates fire when a unique key part is NULL?`

💡 Result:

Yes, both MySQL and PlanetScale UNIQUE indexes allow multiple NULL values in indexed columns because NULL values are treated as distinct from each other in uniqueness checks. No, duplicate-key updates (ON DUPLICATE KEY UPDATE) do not fire when attempting to insert another row with NULL in the unique key part, as there is no duplicate key violation—multiple NULLs are permitted, resulting in a new row insertion instead of triggering the update clause.

Citations:

- 1: https://oneuptime.REDACTED
- 2: https://dev.mysql.com/doc/refman/en/create-index.html

**The unique constraint cannot protect null-division scores because `scaling_level_id` is nullable.**

MySQL unique indexes allow multiple `NULL` values, so rows with `(competition_event_id, user_id, NULL)` do not trigger duplicate-key violations. This means `.onDuplicateKeyUpdate()` will not fire when `scaling_level_id` is `NULL`, causing duplicate score rows for athletes without a division assignment instead of updating the existing row. 🌐 Web query:

`In MySQL 8 / PlanetScale, do UNIQUE indexes allow multiple rows when one indexed column is NULL, and does INSERT ... ON DUPLICATE KEY UPDATE fire for duplicate key values containing NULL?`

💡 Result:

Yes, in MySQL 8 and PlanetScale (Vitess-based), UNIQUE indexes allow multiple rows with NULL in one indexed column because NULL values are not considered equal, permitting multiple NULLs. INSERT ... ON DUPLICATE KEY UPDATE does NOT fire for "duplicate" keys containing NULL, as NULL != NULL, so no duplicate key error occurs and it inserts a new row instead of updating.

Citations:

- 1: https://dev.mysql.com/doc/en/create-index.html
- 2: https://dev.mysql.com/doc/refman/8.0/en/create-index.html
- 3: https://stackoverflow.com/questions/58267590/how-to-make-unique-index-constraints-on-multiple-nullable-columns
- 4: https://bugs.mysql.com/bug.php?id=8173

**Silent-null fallthrough when `requestedDivisionId` doesn't match any registration.**

If the caller passes a `requestedDivisionId` that the athlete is *not* actually registered in, the lookup on Lines 250–261 returns no row and `resolveAthleteDivisionId` returns `null`. In `getAthleteEventScoreFn` (Lines 325–327) this means the `scalingLevelId` predicate is dropped and the query becomes `(event, user)` only — so an athlete querying "get my score for division X" when they're not in division X will still receive a score from whichever other division they *are* registered in (whatever the DB picks via `.limit(1)`). That's a subtle cross-division leak on the read path that the PR is otherwise careful to prevent.

Two options:
- Throw/return a well-defined empty result when the requested registration isn't found, rather than falling back to unscoped.
- Return a sentinel the caller can distinguish from "user has exactly one registration," and have `getAthleteEventScoreFn` treat the mismatch as "no score".

<details>
<summary>🔒 Proposed fix (sketch)</summary>

```diff
 if (requestedDivisionId) {
   const [reg] = await db
     .select({ divisionId: competitionRegistrationsTable.divisionId })
     .from(competitionRegistrationsTable)
     .where(
       and(
         ...baseConditions,
         eq(competitionRegistrationsTable.divisionId, requestedDivisionId),
       ),
     )
     .limit(1)
-  return reg?.divisionId ?? null
+  if (!reg) {
+    throw new Error(
+      "Athlete is not registered in the requested division",
+    )
+  }
+  return reg.divisionId
 }
```

**Scope open/null registrations with `isNull` to prevent score leakage on shared workouts.**

For registrations without `divisionId`, the score fetch and delete queries match only on `(userId, competitionEventId)` without filtering by `scalingLevelId`. This allows open registrations to see or delete a score from any division on shared workouts, causing data leakage across division views.

The schema documents this design intent: the unique constraint on scores is `(competitionEventId, userId, scalingLevelId)` specifically to scope one score per user per event per division. `scalingLevelId` is nullable and represents unscoped scores for open registrations.

<details>
<summary>Proposed fix</summary>

{"author":"coderabbitai[bot]","body":"_⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🔴 Critical_

<details>
<summary>🧩 Analysis chain</summary>

🌐 Web query:

`Do MySQL and PlanetScale UNIQUE indexes allow multiple NULL values in indexed columns, and do duplicate-key updates fire when a unique key part is NULL?`

💡 Result:

Yes, both MySQL and PlanetScale UNIQUE indexes allow multiple NULL values in indexed columns because NULL values are treated as distinct from each other in uniqueness checks. No, duplicate-key updates (ON DUPLICATE KEY UPDATE) do not fire when attempting to insert another row with NULL in the unique key part, as there is no duplicate key violation—multiple NULLs are permitted, resulting in a new row insertion instead of triggering the update clause.

Citations:

- 1: https://oneuptime.REDACTED
- 2: https://dev.mysql.com/doc/refman/en/create-index.html
- 3: https://dev.mysql.com/doc/refman/8.4/en/create-index.html
- 4: https://stackoverflow.com/questions/1298105/mysql-on-duplicate-key-update-with-nullable-column-in-unique-key
- 5: https://stackoverflow.REDACTED

---

1	/**
2	 * Scores Schema
3	 *
4	 * A new unified scoring system that replaces the results + sets tables.
5	 * Designed for efficient sorting and querying.
6	 */
7	
8	import { createId } from "@paralleldrive/cuid2"
9	import { relations } from "drizzle-orm"
10	import {
11	  boolean,
12	  datetime,
13	  index,
14	  int,
15	  mysqlTable,
16	  text,
17	  uniqueIndex,
18	  varchar,
19	} from "drizzle-orm/mysql-core"
20	import { commonColumns } from "./common"
21	import { scalingLevelsTable } from "./scaling"
22	import { teamTable } from "./teams"
23	import { userTable } from "./users"
24	import { workouts } from "./workouts"
25	
26	// ID generators
27	export const createScoreId = () => `score_${createId()}`
28	export const createScoreRoundId = () => `scrd_${createId()}`
29	export const createScoreVerificationLogId = () => `svlog_${createId()}`
30	
31	// Score status values for the new scores table
32	// This is a subset focused on competition/scoring context
33	const SCORE_STATUS_NEW_VALUES = ["scored", "cap", "dq", "withdrawn"] as const
34	export type ScoreStatusNew = (typeof SCORE_STATUS_NEW_VALUES)[number]
35	
36	/**
37	 * Core scores table
38	 *
39	 * Stores workout results with encoded integer values for efficient sorting.
40	 * Replaces the old results + sets tables.
41	 */
42	export const scoresTable = mysqlTable(
43	  "scores",
44	  {
45	    ...commonColumns,
46	    id: varchar({ length: 255 }).primaryKey().$defaultFn(createScoreId),
47	
48	    // Ownership
49	    userId: varchar({ length: 255 }).notNull(),
50	    teamId: varchar({ length: 255 }).notNull(),
51	
52	    // What was scored
53	    workoutId: varchar({ length: 255 }).notNull(),
54	    competitionEventId: varchar({ length: 255 }), // NULL for personal logs
55	    scheduledWorkoutInstanceId: varchar({ length: 255 }),
56	
57	    // Score classification
58	    scheme: varchar({ length: 255 }).notNull(),
59	    scoreType: varchar({ length: 255 }).notNull().default("max"),
60	
61	    // Primary score (encoded as integer based on scheme)
62	    // Time: milliseconds, Rounds+Reps: rounds*100000+reps, Load: grams, Distance: mm
63	    scoreValue: int(),
64	
65	    // Tiebreak
66	    tiebreakScheme: varchar({ length: 255 }),
67	    tiebreakValue: int(),
68	
69	    // Time cap handling (for time-with-cap workouts)
70	    timeCapMs: int(),
71	    // Note: secondaryScheme removed - when capped, score is always reps
72	    secondaryValue: int(), // reps completed if capped
73	
74	    // Status & sorting
75	    status: varchar({ length: 255 }).notNull().default("scored"),
76	    statusOrder: int().notNull().default(0), // 0=scored, 1=cap, 2=dq, 3=withdrawn
77	    // Compound sort key: encodes status + normalized score for single-column sorting
78	    sortKey: varchar({ length: 255 }),
79	
80	    // Scaling
81	    scalingLevelId: varchar({ length: 255 }),
82	    asRx: boolean().notNull().default(false),
83	
84	    // Metadata
85	    notes: text(),
86	    // When the workout was performed (Unix timestamp ms)
87	    recordedAt: datetime().notNull(),
88	
89	    // Verification (organizer review for online competitions)
90	    // null = unreviewed, "verified" = confirmed, "adjusted" = overridden, "invalid" = zeroed
91	    verificationStatus: varchar({ length: 20 }),
92	    verifiedAt: datetime(),
93	    verifiedByUserId: varchar({ length: 255 }),
94	
95	    // Penalty classification (denormalized from verification log for leaderboard/athlete display)
96	    // null = no penalty, "minor" | "major"
97	    penaltyType: varchar("penalty_type", { length: 20 }),
98	    // The percentage deduction applied (0-100)
99	    penaltyPercentage: int("penalty_percentage"),
100	    // Total no-rep count from review notes
101	    noRepCount: int("no_rep_count"),
102	  },
103	  (table) => [
104	    // User's scores, ordered by date
105	    index("idx_scores_user").on(table.userId, table.recordedAt),
106	    // Leaderboard queries: workout + team, sorted by status and score
107	    index("idx_scores_workout").on(
108	      table.workoutId,
109	      table.teamId,
110	      table.statusOrder,
111	      table.sortKey,
112	    ),
113	    // Competition leaderboards
114	    index("idx_scores_competition").on(
115	      table.competitionEventId,
116	      table.statusOrder,
117	      table.sortKey,
118	    ),
119	    // Scheduled workout lookup
120	    index("idx_scores_scheduled").on(table.scheduledWorkoutInstanceId),
121	    // Unique constraint for competition scores: one score per user per event per division.
122	    // Scoping by scalingLevelId lets an athlete registered in multiple divisions
123	    // (e.g. individual + partner) hold a separate score for a workout shared
124	    // across divisions — without scalingLevelId here, the upsert merges the two
125	    // divisions' scores into one row and leaks across leaderboards.
126	    uniqueIndex("idx_scores_competition_user_unique").on(
127	      table.competitionEventId,
128	      table.userId,
129	      table.scalingLevelId,
130	    ),
131	  ],
132	)
133	
134	/**
135	 * Score rounds table
136	 *
137	 * Stores individual rounds/sets within a score.
138	 * For multi-round workouts like "10x3 Back Squat" or "3 rounds for time".
139	 */
140	export const scoreRoundsTable = mysqlTable(
141	  "score_rounds",
142	  {
143	    id: varchar({ length: 255 }).primaryKey().$defaultFn(createScoreRoundId),
144	    scoreId: varchar({ length: 255 }).notNull(),
145	
146	    // Round ordering (1-indexed)
147	    roundNumber: int().notNull(),
148	
149	    // The value for this round (encoded based on parent score's scheme)
150	    value: int().notNull(),
151	
152	    // Optional: different scheme per round (rare, but possible)
153	    schemeOverride: varchar({ length: 255 }),
154	
155	    // Status for this specific round
156	    status: varchar({ length: 255 }),
157	
158	    // For time-capped rounds
159	    secondaryValue: int(),
160	
161	    // Metadata
162	    notes: text(),
163	    createdAt: datetime()
164	      .$defaultFn(() => new Date())
165	      .notNull(),
166	  },
167	  (table) => [
168	    // Lookup rounds for a score
169	    index("idx_score_rounds_score").on(table.scoreId, table.roundNumber),
170	    // Unique constraint on score + round number
171	    uniqueIndex("idx_score_rounds_unique").on(table.scoreId, table.roundNumber),
172	  ],
173	)
174	
175	// Relations
176	export const scoresRelations = relations(scoresTable, ({ one, many }) => ({
177	  user: one(userTable, {
178	    fields: [scoresTable.userId],
179	    references: [userTable.id],
180	  }),
181	  team: one(teamTable, {
182	    fields: [scoresTable.teamId],
183	    references: [teamTable.id],
184	  }),
185	  workout: one(workouts, {
186	    fields: [scoresTable.workoutId],
187	    references: [workouts.id],
188	  }),
189	  scalingLevel: one(scalingLevelsTable, {
190	    fields: [scoresTable.scalingLevelId],
191	    references: [scalingLevelsTable.id],
192	  }),
193	  rounds: many(scoreRoundsTable),
194	}))
195	
196	export const scoreRoundsRelations = relations(scoreRoundsTable, ({ one }) => ({
197	  score: one(scoresTable, {
198	    fields: [scoreRoundsTable.scoreId],
199	    references: [scoresTable.id],
200	  }),
201	}))
202	
203	/**
204	 * Score verification log table
205	 *
206	 * Append-only audit trail of every organizer verify/adjust action on a score.
207	 * For "adjusted" entries, captures the original values before overwrite so
208	 * the athlete's claimed score is never permanently lost.
209	 */
210	export const scoreVerificationLogsTable = mysqlTable(
211	  "score_verification_logs",
212	  {
213	    id: varchar({ length: 255 })
214	      .primaryKey()
215	      .$defaultFn(createScoreVerificationLogId),
216	
217	    // The score that was acted on
218	    scoreId: varchar({ length: 255 }).notNull(),
219	
220	    // Competition context (denormalized for easy querying, null for non-competition scores)
221	    competitionId: varchar({ length: 255 }),
222	    trackWorkoutId: varchar({ length: 255 }),
223	
224	    // The athlete who owns the score
225	    athleteUserId: varchar({ length: 255 }).notNull(),
226	
227	    // What the organizer did
228	    action: varchar({ length: 20 }).notNull(), // "verified" | "adjusted" | "invalid"
229	
230	    // Values before adjustment (null for "verified" action)
231	    originalScoreValue: int(),
232	    originalStatus: varchar({ length: 50 }),
233	    originalSecondaryValue: int(),
234	    originalTiebreakValue: int(),
235	
236	    // Values after adjustment (null for "verified" action)
237	    newScoreValue: int(),
238	    newStatus: varchar({ length: 50 }),
239	    newSecondaryValue: int(),
240	    newTiebreakValue: int(),
241	
242	    // Penalty audit trail (snapshot at time of action)
243	    penaltyType: varchar("penalty_type", { length: 20 }),
244	    penaltyPercentage: int("penalty_percentage"),
245	    noRepCount: int("no_rep_count"),
246	
247	    // Who did it and when
248	    performedByUserId: varchar({ length: 255 }).notNull(),
249	    performedAt: datetime().notNull(),
250	  },
251	  (table) => [
252	    // Look up all log entries for a score
253	    index("idx_svlog_score").on(table.scoreId),
254	    // Look up all actions by an organizer
255	    index("idx_svlog_performer").on(table.performedByUserId, table.performedAt),
256	  ],
257	)
258	
259	export const scoreVerificationLogsRelations = relations(
260	  scoreVerificationLogsTable,
261	  ({ one }) => ({
262	    score: one(scoresTable, {
263	      fields: [scoreVerificationLogsTable.scoreId],
264	      references: [scoresTable.id],
265	    }),
266	    performedBy: one(userTable, {
267	      fields: [scoreVerificationLogsTable.performedByUserId],
268	      references: [userTable.id],
269	    }),
270	  }),
271	)
272	
273	// Type exports
274	export type Score = typeof scoresTable.$inferSelect
275	export type ScoreInsert = typeof scoresTable.$inferInsert
276	export type ScoreRound = typeof scoreRoundsTable.$inferSelect
277	export type ScoreRoundInsert = typeof scoreRoundsTable.$inferInsert
278

content.content:
1	-- Widen the scoresTable unique index so one athlete can hold a distinct score
2	-- per division on a shared workout. Before: unique(competition_event_id, user_id)
3	-- collapsed an athlete's individual + partner scores into one row via
4	-- onDuplicateKeyUpdate, and every leaderboard/athlete-detail read keyed by
5	-- (event, user) pulled that single row onto every registration the user owned.
6	--
7	-- Column-name note: `scores.competition_event_id` is misleadingly named — it
8	-- actually stores a `track_workouts.id`, not a `competition_events.id`. Every
9	-- insert assigns it from the caller's `trackWorkoutId`. That's exactly the
10	-- right scope here: when the same workout appears in multiple divisions via
11	-- event-division mappings, there is one track_workout row referenced by both
12	-- divisions, so the unique key `(track_workout_id, user_id, scaling_level_id)`
13	-- is what lets a multi-division athlete hold one score per division.
14	--
15	-- Safe to run: the existing 2-column unique guarantees at most one row per
16	-- (track_workout, user) pair, so widening to 3 columns cannot produce
17	-- duplicates on existing data.
18	
19	ALTER TABLE `scores` DROP INDEX `idx_scores_competition_user_unique`;
20	
21	ALTER TABLE `scores`
22	  ADD CONSTRAINT `idx_scores_competition_user_unique`
23	  UNIQUE (`competition_event_id`, `user_id`, `scaling_level_id`);
24

file.content:
-- Widen the scoresTable unique index so one athlete can hold a distinct score
-- per division on a shared workout. Before: unique(competition_event_id, user_id)
-- collapsed an athlete's individual + partner scores into one row via
-- onDuplicateKeyUpdate, and every leaderboard/athlete-detail read keyed by
-- (event, user) pulled that single row onto every registration the user owned.
--
-- Column-name note: `scores.competition_event_id` is misleadingly named — it
-- actually stores a `track_workouts.id`, not a `competition_events.id`. Every
-- insert assigns it from the caller's `trackWorkoutId`. That's exactly the
-- right scope here: when the same workout appears in multiple divisions via
-- event-division mappings, there is one track_workout row referenced by both
-- divisions, so the unique key `(track_workout_id, user_id, scaling_level_id)`
-- is what lets a multi-division athlete hold one score per division.
--
-- Safe to run: the existing 2-column unique guarantees at most one row per
-- (track_workout, user) pair, so widening to 3 columns cannot produce
-- duplicates on existing data.

ALTER TABLE `scores` DROP INDEX `idx_scores_competition_user_unique`;

ALTER TABLE `scores`
  ADD CONSTRAINT `idx_scores_competition_user_unique`
  UNIQUE (`competition_event_id`, `user_id`, `scaling_level_id`);

content.content:
1	/**
2	 * Athlete Score Submission Server Functions
3	 *
4	 * Server functions for athletes to submit their own scores in online competitions.
5	 * Includes validation for submission windows and score format based on workout scheme.
6	 *
7	 * OBSERVABILITY:
8	 * - All athlete score submissions are logged with user and event IDs
9	 * - Submission window status checks are logged
10	 * - Validation failures tracked for debugging
11	 */
12	
13	import { createServerFn } from "@tanstack/react-start"
14	import { and, eq, ne } from "drizzle-orm"
15	import { z } from "zod"
16	import { getDb } from "@/db"
17	import {
18	  addRequestContextAttribute,
19	  logEntityUpdated,
20	  logError,
21	  logInfo,
22	  logWarning,
23	  updateRequestContext,
24	} from "@/lib/logging"
25	import {
26	  competitionEventsTable,
27	  competitionRegistrationsTable,
28	  competitionsTable,
29	  REGISTRATION_STATUS,
30	} from "@/db/schemas/competitions"
31	import {
32	  programmingTracksTable,
33	  trackWorkoutsTable,
34	} from "@/db/schemas/programming"
35	import { scoresTable } from "@/db/schemas/scores"
36	import type { TiebreakScheme } from "@/db/schemas/workouts"
37	import { workouts } from "@/db/schemas/workouts"
38	import {
39	  computeSortKey,
40	  encodeScore,
41	  getDefaultScoreType,
42	  parseScore,
43	  type ScoreType,
44	  STATUS_ORDER,
45	  sortKeyToString,
46	  type WorkoutScheme,
47	} from "@/lib/scoring"
48	import { getSessionFromCookie } from "@/utils/auth"
49	
50	// ============================================================================
51	// Types
52	// ============================================================================
53

57	  status: "scored" | "cap"

59	  secondaryScore?: string

/**
 * Athlete Score Submission Server Functions
 *
 * Server functions for athletes to submit their own scores in online competitions.
 * Includes validation for submission windows and score format based on workout scheme.
 *
 * OBSERVABILITY:
 * - All athlete score submissions are logged with user and event IDs
 * - Submission window status checks are logged
 * - Validation failures tracked for debugging
 */

import { createServerFn } from "@tanstack/react-start"
import { and, eq, ne } from "drizzle-orm"
import { z } from "zod"
import { getDb } from "@/db"
import {
  addRequestContextAttribute,
  logEntityUpdated,
  logError,
  logInfo,
  logWarning,
  updateRequestContext,
} from "@/lib/logging"
import {
  competitionEventsTable,
  competitionRegistrationsTable,
  competitionsTable,
  REGISTRATION_STATUS,
} from "@/db/schemas/competitions"
import {
  programmingTracksTable,
  trackWorkoutsTable,
} from "@/db/schemas/programming"
import { scoresTable } from "@/db/schemas/scores"
import type { TiebreakScheme } from "@/db/schemas/workouts"
import { workouts } from "@/db/schemas/workouts"
import {
  computeSortKey,
  encodeScore,
  getDefaultScoreType,
  parseScore,
  type ScoreType,
  STATUS_ORDER,
  sortKeyToString,
  type WorkoutScheme,
} from "@/lib/scoring"
import { getSessionFromCookie } from "@/utils/auth"

// ============================================================================
// Types
// ============================================================================

export interface AthleteScoreSubmission {
  trackWorkoutId: string
  score: string
  status: "scored" | "cap"
  /** Secondary score (reps at cap) for time-capped workouts */
  secondaryScore?: string
  /** Tiebreak score if applicable */
  tiebreakScore?: string
}

export interface SubmissionWindowStatus {
  isOpen: boolean
  opensAt: string | null
  closesAt: string | null
  reason?: string
}

export interface AthleteEventScore {
  scoreId: string | null
  scoreValue: number | null
  displayScore: string | null
  status: string | null
  secondaryValue: number | null
  tiebreakValue: number | null
  submittedAt: Date | null
}

content.content:
200	
201	  if (now < opensAt) {
202	    return {
203	      isOpen: false,
204	      opensAt: event.submissionOpensAt,
205	      closesAt: event.submissionClosesAt,
206	      reason: "Submission window has not opened yet",
207	    }
208	  }
209	
210	  if (now > closesAt) {
211	    return {
212	      isOpen: false,
213	      opensAt: event.submissionOpensAt,
214	      closesAt: event.submissionClosesAt,
215	      reason: "Submission window has closed",
216	    }
217	  }
218	
219	  return {
220	    isOpen: true,
221	    opensAt: event.submissionOpensAt,
222	    closesAt: event.submissionClosesAt,
223	  }
224	}
225	
226	/**
227	 * Resolve which divisionId to scope a score read/write to. When the caller
228	 * supplies a divisionId, verify the user is actually registered in it. Otherwise,
229	 * if the user has exactly one active registration, use that division. If they
230	 * have multiple registrations and none was specified, return null so the caller
231	 * can decide how to handle the ambiguity (error vs. "any of their divisions").
232	 */
233	async function resolveAthleteDivisionId({
234	  competitionId,
235	  userId,
236	  requestedDivisionId,
237	}: {
238	  competitionId: string
239	  userId: string
240	  requestedDivisionId?: string
241	}): Promise<string | null> {
242	  const db = getDb()
243	
244	  const baseConditions = [
245	    eq(competitionRegistrationsTable.eventId, competitionId),
246	    eq(competitionRegistrationsTable.userId, userId),
247	    ne(competitionRegistrationsTable.status, REGISTRATION_STATUS.REMOVED),
248	  ]
249	
250	  if (requestedDivisionId) {
251	    const [reg] = await db
252	      .select({ divisionId: competitionRegistrationsTable.divisionId })
253	      .from(competitionRegistrationsTable)
254	      .where(
255	        and(
256	          ...baseConditions,
257	          eq(competitionRegistrationsTable.divisionId, requestedDivisionId),
258	        ),
259	      )
260	      .limit(1)
261	    return reg?.divisionId ?? null
262	  }
263	
264	  const regs = await db
265	    .select({ divisionId: competitionRegistrationsTable.divisionId })
266	    .from(competitionRegistrationsTable)
267	    .where(and(...baseConditions))
268	    .limit(2)
269	
270	  if (regs.length === 1) return regs[0].divisionId ?? null
271	  return null
272	}
273	
274	// ============================================================================
275	// Server Functions
276	// ============================================================================
277	
278	/**
279	 * Get the submission window status for an event
280	 */
281	export const getSubmissionWindowStatusFn = createServerFn({ method: "GET" })
282	  .inputValidator((data: unknown) => getSubmissionWindowInputSchema.parse(data))
283	  .handler(async ({ data }): Promise<SubmissionWindowStatus> => {
284	    return checkSubmissionWindow(data.competitionId, data.trackWorkoutId)
285	  })
286	
287	/**
288	 * Get the athlete's existing score for an event
289	 */
290	export const getAthleteEventScoreFn = createServerFn({ method: "GET" })
291	  .inputValidator((data: unknown) => getScoreInputSchema.parse(data))
292	  .handler(async ({ data }): Promise<AthleteEventScore> => {
293	    const session = await getSessionFromCookie()
294	    if (!session?.userId) {
295	      logWarning({
296	        message: "[AthleteScore] Get score denied - not authenticated",
297	        attributes: {
298	          competitionId: data.competitionId,
299	          trackWorkoutId: data.trackWorkoutId,
300	        },
301	      })
302	      throw new Error("Not authenticated")
303	    }
304	
305	    // Update request context
306	    updateRequestContext({ userId: session.userId })
307	    addRequestContextAttribute("competitionId", data.competitionId)
308	    addRequestContextAttribute("trackWorkoutId", data.trackWorkoutId)
309	
310	    const db = getDb()
311	
312	    // Resolve the athlete's division for this competition. When an athlete is
313	    // registered in multiple divisions that share a workout, scores are keyed
314	    // per-division — pick the right row by scoping to divisionId.
315	    const resolvedDivisionId = await resolveAthleteDivisionId({
316	      competitionId: data.competitionId,
317	      userId: session.userId,
318	      requestedDivisionId: data.divisionId,
319	    })
320	
321	    const scoreConditions = [
322	      eq(scoresTable.competitionEventId, data.trackWorkoutId),
323	      eq(scoresTable.userId, session.userId),
324	    ]
325	    if (resolvedDivisionId) {
326	      scoreConditions.push(eq(scoresTable.scalingLevelId, resolvedDivisionId))
327	    }
328	
329	    // Get the user's existing score for this event (division-scoped)
330	    const [existingScore] = await db
331	      .select({
332	        id: scoresTable.id,
333	        scoreValue: scoresTable.scoreValue,
334	        status: scoresTable.status,
335	        secondaryValue: scoresTable.secondaryValue,
336	        tiebreakValue: scoresTable.tiebreakValue,
337	        recordedAt: scoresTable.recordedAt,
338	        scheme: scoresTable.scheme,
339	      })
340	      .from(scoresTable)
341	      .where(and(...scoreConditions))
342	      .limit(1)
343	
344	    if (!existingScore) {
345	      return {
346	        scoreId: null,
347	        scoreValue: null,
348	        displayScore: null,
349	        status: null,
350	        secondaryValue: null,
351	        tiebreakValue: null,
352	        submittedAt: null,
353	      }
354	    }
355	
356	    // Decode the score for display
357	    const { decodeScore } = await import("@/lib/scoring")
358	    let displayScore: string | null = null
359	    if (existingScore.scoreValue !== null) {
360	      displayScore = decodeScore(
361	        existingScore.scoreValue,
362	        existingScore.scheme as WorkoutScheme,
363	        { compact: false },
364	      )
365	    }
366	
367	    return {
368	      scoreId: existingScore.id,
369	      scoreValue: existingScore.scoreValue,
370	      displayScore,
371	      status: existingScore.status,
372	      secondaryValue: existingScore.secondaryValue,
373	      tiebreakValue: existingScore.tiebreakValue,
374	      submittedAt: existingScore.recordedAt,
375	    }
376	  })
377	
378	/**
379	 * Submit or update an athlete's score for an event
380	 */
381	export const submitAthleteScoreFn = createServerFn({ method: "POST" })
382	  .inputValidator((data: unknown) => submitScoreInputSchema.parse(data))
383	  .handler(
384	    async ({
385	      data,
386	    }): Promise<{
387	      success: boolean
388	      scoreId: string
389	      message: string
390	    }> => {
391	      const session = await getSessionFromCookie()
392	      if (!session?.userId) {
393	        logWarning({
394	          message: "[AthleteScore] Submission denied - not authenticated",
395	          attributes: {
396	            competitionId: data.competitionId,
397	            trackWorkoutId: data.trackWorkoutId,
398	          },
399	        })
400	        throw new Error("Not authenticated")
401	      }
402	
403	      const db = getDb()
404	
405	      // Update request context
406	      updateRequestContext({ userId: session.userId })
407	      addRequestContextAttribute("competitionId", data.competitionId)
408	      addRequestContextAttribute("trackWorkoutId", data.trackWorkoutId)
409	
410	      logInfo({
411	        message: "[AthleteScore] Score submission started",
412	        attributes: {
413	          competitionId: data.competitionId,
414	          trackWorkoutId: data.trackWorkoutId,
415	          userId: session.userId,
416	          status: data.status,
417	        },
418	      })
419	
420	      // 1. Resolve the registration scoped to the division the athlete is
421	      // submitting for. If divisionId is provided, match it exactly. Otherwise,
422	      // only proceed when the user has a single registration — ambiguity between
423	      // multi-division registrations would silently write to the wrong division.
424	      const regConditions = [
425	        eq(competitionRegistrationsTable.eventId, data.competitionId),
426	        eq(competitionRegistrationsTable.userId, session.userId),
427	        ne(competitionRegistrationsTable.status, REGISTRATION_STATUS.REMOVED),
428	      ]
429	      if (data.divisionId) {
430	        regConditions.push(
431	          eq(competitionRegistrationsTable.divisionId, data.divisionId),
432	        )
433	      }
434	
435	      const registrations = await db
436	        .select({
437	          id: competitionRegistrationsTable.id,
438	          divisionId: competitionRegistrationsTable.divisionId,
439	        })
440	        .from(competitionRegistrationsTable)
441	        .where(and(...regConditions))
442	        .limit(2)
443	
444	      if (registrations.length === 0) {
445	        logWarning({
446	          message: "[AthleteScore] Submission denied - not registered",
447	          attributes: {
448	            competitionId: data.competitionId,
449	            trackWorkoutId: data.trackWorkoutId,
450	            userId: session.userId,
451	            divisionId: data.divisionId ?? null,
452	          },
453	        })
454	        throw new Error("You are not registered for this competition")
455	      }
456	
457	      if (registrations.length > 1) {
458	        logWarning({
459	          message: "[AthleteScore] Submission denied - ambiguous division",
460	          attributes: {
461	            competitionId: data.competitionId,
462	            trackWorkoutId: data.trackWorkoutId,
463	            userId: session.userId,
464	          },
465	        })
466	        throw new Error(
467	          "You are registered in multiple divisions for this competition. Please specify which division to submit for.",
468	        )
469	      }
470	
471	      const registration = registrations[0]
472	      addRequestContextAttribute("registrationId", registration.id)
473	
474	      // 2. Check submission window
475	      const windowStatus = await checkSubmissionWindow(
476	        data.competitionId,
477	        data.trackWorkoutId,
478	      )
479	
480	      if (!windowStatus.isOpen) {
481	        logWarning({
482	          message: "[AthleteScore] Submission window blocked",
483	          attributes: {
484	            competitionId: data.competitionId,
485	            trackWorkoutId: data.trackWorkoutId,
486	            userId: session.userId,
487	            reason: windowStatus.reason,
488	            opensAt: windowStatus.opensAt,
489	            closesAt: windowStatus.closesAt,
490	          },
491	        })
492	        throw new Error(windowStatus.reason || "Submission window is not open")
493	      }
494	
495	      // 3. Get workout info for proper encoding
496	      const [trackWorkout] = await db
497	        .select({
498	          workoutId: trackWorkoutsTable.workoutId,
499	          trackId: trackWorkoutsTable.trackId,
500	        })
501	        .from(trackWorkoutsTable)
502	        .where(eq(trackWorkoutsTable.id, data.trackWorkoutId))
503	        .limit(1)
504	
505	      if (!trackWorkout) {
506	        throw new Error("Event not found")
507	      }
508	
509	      const [workout] = await db
510	        .select({
511	          scheme: workouts.scheme,
512	          scoreType: workouts.scoreType,
513	          tiebreakScheme: workouts.tiebreakScheme,
514	          timeCap: workouts.timeCap,
515	        })
516	        .from(workouts)
517	        .where(eq(workouts.id, trackWorkout.workoutId))
518	        .limit(1)
519	
520	      if (!workout) {
521	        throw new Error("Workout not found")
522	      }
523	
524	      const scheme = workout.scheme as WorkoutScheme
525	      const scoreType =
526	        (workout.scoreType as ScoreType) || getDefaultScoreType(scheme)
527	
528	      // 4. Parse and validate the score
529	      const parseResult = parseScore(data.score, scheme)
530	      if (!parseResult.isValid) {
531	        logWarning({
532	          message: "[AthleteScore] Invalid score format",
533	          attributes: {
534	            competitionId: data.competitionId,
535	            trackWorkoutId: data.trackWorkoutId,
536	            userId: session.userId,
537	            score: data.score,
538	            scheme,
539	            error: parseResult.error,
540	          },
541	        })
542	        throw new Error(
543	          `Invalid score format: ${parseResult.error || "Please check your entry"}`,
544	        )
545	      }
546	
547	      // Encode the score
548	      let encodedValue: number | null = encodeScore(data.score, scheme)
549	
550	      // Handle CAP status for time-with-cap workouts
551	      if (
552	        data.status === "cap" &&
553	        scheme === "time-with-cap" &&
554	        workout.timeCap
555	      ) {
556	        encodedValue = workout.timeCap * 1000 // Time cap in milliseconds
557	      }
558	
559	      // Parse secondary score (reps at cap)
560	      let secondaryValue: number | null = null
561	      if (data.secondaryScore && data.status === "cap") {
562	        const parsed = Number.parseInt(data.secondaryScore.trim(), 10)
563	        if (!Number.isNaN(parsed) && parsed >= 0) {
564	          secondaryValue = parsed
565	        }
566	      }
567	
568	      // Parse tiebreak score
569	      let tiebreakValue: number | null = null
570	      if (data.tiebreakScore && workout.tiebreakScheme) {
571	        tiebreakValue = encodeScore(
572	          data.tiebreakScore,
573	          workout.tiebreakScheme as WorkoutScheme,
574	        )
575	      }
576	
577	      // Time cap in milliseconds
578	      const timeCapMs = workout.timeCap ? workout.timeCap * 1000 : null
579	
580	      // Compute sort key
581	      const sortKey =
582	        encodedValue !== null
583	          ? computeSortKey({
584	              value: encodedValue,
585	              status: data.status,
586	              scheme,
587	              scoreType,
588	            })
589	          : null
590	
591	      // 5. Get teamId from track
592	      const [track] = await db
593	        .select({
594	          ownerTeamId: programmingTracksTable.ownerTeamId,
595	        })
596	        .from(programmingTracksTable)
597	        .where(eq(programmingTracksTable.id, trackWorkout.trackId))
598	        .limit(1)
599	
600	      if (!track?.ownerTeamId) {
601	        throw new Error("Could not determine team ownership")
602	      }
603	
604	      // 6. Upsert the score
605	      await db
606	        .insert(scoresTable)
607	        .values({
608	          userId: session.userId,
609	          teamId: track.ownerTeamId,
610	          workoutId: trackWorkout.workoutId,
611	          competitionEventId: data.trackWorkoutId,
612	          scheme,
613	          scoreType,
614	          scoreValue: encodedValue,
615	          status: data.status,
616	          statusOrder: getStatusOrder(data.status),
617	          sortKey: sortKey ? sortKeyToString(sortKey) : null,
618	          tiebreakScheme: (workout.tiebreakScheme as TiebreakScheme) ?? null,
619	          tiebreakValue,
620	          timeCapMs,
621	          secondaryValue,
622	          scalingLevelId: registration.divisionId,
623	          asRx: true,
624	          recordedAt: new Date(),
625	        })
626	        .onDuplicateKeyUpdate({
627	          set: {
628	            scoreValue: encodedValue,
629	            status: data.status,
630	            statusOrder: getStatusOrder(data.status),
631	            sortKey: sortKey ? sortKeyToString(sortKey) : null,
632	            tiebreakScheme: (workout.tiebreakScheme as TiebreakScheme) ?? null,
633	            tiebreakValue,
634	            timeCapMs,
635	            secondaryValue,
636	            scalingLevelId: registration.divisionId,
637	            updatedAt: new Date(),
638	          },
639	        })
640	
641	      // Get the final score ID — scope by division to match the 3-col unique key
642	      const finalScoreConditions = [
643	        eq(scoresTable.competitionEventId, data.trackWorkoutId),
644	        eq(scoresTable.userId, session.userId),
645	      ]
646	      if (registration.divisionId) {
647	        finalScoreConditions.push(
648	          eq(scoresTable.scalingLevelId, registration.divisionId),
649	        )

651	      const [finalScore] = await db
652	        .select({ id: scoresTable.id })

701	/**
702	 * Get workout details needed for score submission UI
703	 */
704	export const getEventWorkoutDetailsFn = createServerFn({ method: "GET" })
705	  .inputValidator((data: unknown) => getScoreInputSchema.parse(data))
706	  .handler(
707	    async ({
708	      data,
709	    }): Promise<{
710	      workoutId: string
711	      name: string
712	      scheme: WorkoutScheme
713	      scoreType: ScoreType | null

717	    }> => {

735	      if (!result) {

737	      }
738	
739	      return {

744	        timeCap: result.timeCap,
745	        tiebreakScheme: result.tiebreakScheme,
746	        repsPerRound: result.repsPerRound,
747	      }
748	    },
749	  )

80	
81	// ============================================================================
82	// Input Schemas
83	// ============================================================================
84	
85	const submitScoreInputSchema = z.object({
86	  competitionId: z.string().min(1),
87	  trackWorkoutId: z.string().min(1),
88	  divisionId: z.string().optional(),
89	  score: z.string().min(1, "Score is required"),
90	  status: z.enum(["scored", "cap"]),
91	  secondaryScore: z.string().optional(),
92	  tiebreakScore: z.string().optional(),
93	})
94	
95	const getScoreInputSchema = z.object({
96	  competitionId: z.string().min(1),
97	  trackWorkoutId: z.string().min(1),
98	  divisionId: z.string().optional(),
99	})
100	
101	const getSubmissionWindowInputSchema = z.object({
102	  competitionId: z.string().min(1),
103	  trackWorkoutId: z.string().min(1),
104	})
105	
106	// ============================================================================
107	// Helper Functions
108	// ============================================================================
109	
110	/**

124	/**
125	 * Check if current time is within the event's submission window.
126	 */
127	async function checkSubmissionWindow(
128	  competitionId: string,
129	  trackWorkoutId: string,
130	): Promise<SubmissionWindowStatus> {
131	  const db = getDb()
132	
133	  // Get competition type
134	  const [competition] = await db
135	    .select({
136	      competitionType: competitionsTable.competitionType,
137	    })
138	    .from(competitionsTable)
139	    .where(eq(competitionsTable.id, competitionId))
140	    .limit(1)
141	
142	  if (!competition) {
143	    return {
144	      isOpen: false,
145	      opensAt: null,
146	      closesAt: null,
147	      reason: "Competition not found",
148	    }
149	  }
150	
151	  // Only check submission windows for online competitions
152	  if (competition.competitionType !== "online") {
153	    return {
154	      isOpen: false,
155	      opensAt: null,
156	      closesAt: null,
157	      reason: "This is not an online competition",
158	    }
159	  }
160	
161	  // Get competition event with submission window
162	  const [event] = await db
163	    .select({
164	      submissionOpensAt: competitionEventsTable.submissionOpensAt,
165	      submissionClosesAt: competitionEventsTable.submissionClosesAt,
166	    })
167	    .from(competitionEventsTable)
168	    .where(
169	      and(
170	        eq(competitionEventsTable.competitionId, competitionId),
171	        eq(competitionEventsTable.trackWorkoutId, trackWorkoutId),
172	      ),
173	    )
174	    .limit(1)
175	
176	  // If no event record exists, submission not configured
177	  if (!event) {
178	    return {
179	      isOpen: false,
180	      opensAt: null,
181	      closesAt: null,
182	      reason: "Submission window not configured",
183	    }
184	  }
185	
186	  // If no submission window is configured, submission not allowed
187	  if (!event.submissionOpensAt || !event.submissionClosesAt) {
188	    return {
189	      isOpen: false,
190	      opensAt: null,
191	      closesAt: null,
192	      reason: "Submission window not configured",
193	    }
194	  }
195	
196	  // Check if current time is within the window
197	  const now = new Date()
198	  const opensAt = new Date(event.submissionOpensAt)
199	  const closesAt = new Date(event.submissionClosesAt)

192	          }

417	export const competitionEventsTable = mysqlTable(
418	  "competition_events",

420	    ...commonColumns,
421	    id: varchar({ length: 255 })
422	      .primaryKey()

424	      .notNull(),

426	    // References track_workout (competition event/workout)
427	    trackWorkoutId: varchar({ length: 255 }).notNull(),
428	    // Submission window for online competitions (ISO 8601 datetime strings)
429	    // Athletes can only submit scores within this window
430	    submissionOpensAt: varchar({ length: 255 }),
431	    submissionClosesAt: varchar({ length: 255 }),
432	  },
433	  (table) => [
434	    index("competition_events_competition_idx").on(table.competitionId),
435	    index("competition_events_workout_idx").on(table.trackWorkoutId),
436	    // One event config per workout per competition
437	    uniqueIndex("competition_events_comp_workout_idx").on(
438	      table.competitionId,
439	      table.trackWorkoutId,

441	  ],

lines: - * Resolve which divisionId to scope a score read/write to. When the caller
- * supplies a divisionId, verify the user is actually registered in it. Otherwise,
- * if the user has exactly one active registration, use that division. If they
- * have multiple registrations and none was specified, return null so the caller
- * can decide how to handle the ambiguity (error vs. "any of their divisions").
+ * Resolution outcome for an athlete's division when reading/writing a score.
+ * Callers handle each kind explicitly so a null divisionId is never silently
+ * collapsed into an unscoped (event, user) query that leaks across divisions.
+  | { kind: "open" } // exactly one registration with a null divisionId
+  | { kind: "ambiguous" } // multiple registrations and caller did not pick one
+  | { kind: "notFound" } // caller asked for a divisionId the user is not in
+ * Resolve which division to scope a score read/write to.
+ * Build the predicate that scopes a score query to a resolved division. Returns
+ * null when the resolved division can't pick a unique row (ambiguous or not
+ * found), letting the caller short-circuit with an empty result instead of
+ * dropping the divisionId predicate and reading from another division.

scoreValue: encodedValue,
            status: newStatus,
            statusOrder: STATUS_ORDER[newStatus],
            sortKey: sortKey ? sortKeyToString(sortKey) : null,
            tiebreakScheme: workoutTiebreakScheme,
            tiebreakValue,
            timeCapMs,
            secondaryValue,
            scalingLevelId: data.divisionId,
            asRx: true,
            recordedAt: new Date(),
          })
          .onDuplicateKeyUpdate({
            set: {
              scoreValue: encodedValue,
              status: newStatus,
              statusOrder: STATUS_ORDER[newStatus],
              sortKey: sortKey ? sortKeyToString(sortKey) : null,
              tiebreakScheme: workoutTiebreakScheme,
              tiebreakValue,
              timeCapMs,
              secondaryValue,
              scalingLevelId: data.divisionId,
              updatedAt: new Date(),
            },
          })

        // Get the final score ID (either new or existing). Scope by division
        // so the correct row is fetched when the athlete has scores in
        // multiple divisions for a workout shared across divisions.
        const finalScoreConditions = [
          eq(scoresTable.competitionEventId, data.trackWorkoutId),
          eq(scoresTable.userId, data.userId),
        ]
        if (data.divisionId) {
          finalScoreConditions.push(
            eq(scoresTable.scalingLevelId, data.divisionId),
          )
        }
        const [finalScore] = await tx
          .select({ id: scoresTable.id })
          .from(scoresTable)
          .where(and(...finalScoreConditions))
          .limit(1)

        if (!finalScore) {
          throw new Error("Failed to retrieve score after upsert")
        }

        const id = finalScore.id

1325	 * Delete a competition score
1326	 */
1327	export const deleteCompetitionScoreFn = createServerFn({ method: "POST" })
1328	  .inputValidator((data: unknown) =>
1329	    deleteCompetitionScoreInputSchema.parse(data),
1330	  )
1331	  .handler(async ({ data }): Promise<{ success: boolean }> => {
1332	    const db = getDb()
1333	
1334	    getEvlog()?.set({
1335	      action: "delete_score",
1336	      score: {
1337	        competitionId: data.competitionId,
1338	        trackWorkoutId: data.trackWorkoutId,
1339	      },
1340	    })
1341	
1342	    // Update request context
1343	    addRequestContextAttribute("competitionId", data.competitionId)
1344	    addRequestContextAttribute("trackWorkoutId", data.trackWorkoutId)
1345	    addRequestContextAttribute("targetUserId", data.userId)
1346	
1347	    logInfo({
1348	      message: "[Score] Delete competition score started",
1349	      attributes: {
1350	        competitionId: data.competitionId,
1351	        trackWorkoutId: data.trackWorkoutId,
1352	        userId: data.userId,
1353	      },
1354	    })
1355	
1356	    // Delete from scores table (score_rounds are cascade deleted). Scope by
1357	    // division so the athlete's scores in other divisions aren't nuked when
1358	    // a workout is shared across divisions.
1359	    const deleteConditions = [
1360	      eq(scoresTable.competitionEventId, data.trackWorkoutId),
1361	      eq(scoresTable.userId, data.userId),
1362	    ]
1363	    if (data.divisionId) {
1364	      deleteConditions.push(eq(scoresTable.scalingLevelId, data.divisionId))
1365	    }
1366	    await db.delete(scoresTable).where(and(...deleteConditions))
1367	
1368	    logEntityDeleted({
1369	      entity: "score",
1370	      id: `${data.trackWorkoutId}:${data.userId}`,
1371	      attributes: {
1372	        competitionId: data.competitionId,
1373	        trackWorkoutId: data.trackWorkoutId,
1374	        userId: data.userId,
1375	      },
1376	    })
1377	
1378	    return { success: true }
1379	  })

571	      let score: number | null = null

574	      if (scheme === "rounds-reps") {
