content.content:
100	The unified scoring system stores competition results in `scoresTable`, replacing a legacy results + sets approach.
101	
102	Key design:
103	- Scores encode values as integers for efficient DB-level sorting
104	- `competitionEventId` links to the competition event (NULL for personal logs)
105	- `scheme` and `scoreType` determine how the raw value is interpreted
106	- Tiebreak values stored separately for time-capped workouts
107	- Score rounds (`scoreRoundsTable`) store per-round breakdowns for multi-round workouts
108	
109	### One score per athlete per event per division
110	
111	The `scoresTable` unique index spans `(competition_event_id, user_id, scaling_level_id)` so an athlete registered in multiple divisions can hold a separate score per division on a shared workout.
112	
113	Column-name note: `scores.competition_event_id` is misleadingly named — it actually stores a `track_workouts.id` (the value the caller supplies as `trackWorkoutId`), not a `competition_events.id`. Every insert path assigns it from `data.trackWorkoutId`. That scope is exactly right for this invariant: when the same workout appears in multiple divisions via event-division mappings, both divisions reference the same `track_workout` row, so the unique key `(track_workout, user, scaling_level)` is what lets a multi-division athlete carry one score per division.
114	
115	Online competitions allow the same track workout to appear in multiple divisions (e.g. scheduled for both Individual and Partner), and athletes can register in more than one division. Keying only on `(track_workout, user)` would merge an athlete's individual and partner scores into one row via `onDuplicateKeyUpdate` — the last submitted score would overwrite the other, and every leaderboard/athlete-detail read (which joins on `track_workout + user`) would surface that single row against every registration the user owned, leaking partner scores onto the individual leaderboard and vice versa. Every score-write path ([[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/video-submission-fns.ts#submitVideoFn]], [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/competition-score-fns.ts#saveCompetitionScoreFn]], [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/athlete-score-fns.ts#submitAthleteScoreFn]], [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/submission-verification-fns.ts]], [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/routes/api/compete/scores/submit.ts]], [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/routes/api/compete/scores/judge.ts]], [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/routes/api/compete/video/submit.ts]]) scopes the registration lookup by divisionId and carries that divisionId through to the upsert's `scalingLevelId`. Submission fns that accept an athlete without a divisionId reject ambiguously when the athlete is registered in more than one division rather than silently picking one. Every score-read path ([[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server/competition-leaderboard.ts#getCompetitionLeaderboard]], [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server/series-leaderboard.ts]], [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/organizer-athlete-fns.ts#getOrganizerAthleteDetailFn]], [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/division-results-fns.ts]]) scopes scores by `scaling_level_id` and groups/attributes by the score's own `scalingLevelId` rather than by the first registration found for that userId — the leaderboard maps each score to the exact `(user, division)` registration, not just userId. Delete paths ([[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/competition-score-fns.ts#deleteCompetitionScoreFn]], [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/registration-fns.ts#removeRegistrationFn]]) scope by division so removing one registration or score doesn't nuke the athlete's scores in their other division on a shared workout. The registration-transfer path in [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server/commerce/transfer-handlers.ts]] intentionally deletes all of the source user's scores across divisions because the user is being fully transferred out of the competition.
116	
117	### Time input parsing matches save semantics
118	
119	Bare numeric input on a time score (no colon — e.g. `2000`) is treated as **raw seconds** in both the live preview and the save path, so the two can never disagree.
120	
121	[[apps/wodsmith-start/src/lib/scoring/parse/time.ts#parseTime]] defers to [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/lib/scoring/encode/time.ts#encodeTime]] for any non-colon input. An earlier version smart-padded bare digits as MM:SS (`2000` → `20:00` in preview) while the save persisted `33:20`, which confused athletes typing on a phone. Colon-delimited input (`12:34`, `1:02:34.567`) is unchanged. Use `precision: "ms"` only when explicitly persisting raw milliseconds.
122	
123	### Missing scores tie at worst place
124	
125	Athletes registered in a division who never submitted a score for an event are awarded the same points as if they finished one place behind every recorded score in their division.
126	
127	If a division has 5 recorded scores for an event, every athlete in that division without a score ties at place 6 and receives the algorithm-appropriate points for place 6 (e.g. 6 points under online scoring, `firstPlacePoints − 5×step` under traditional). [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server/competition-leaderboard.ts#getCompetitionLeaderboard]] computes `missingPlace = activeCount + 1` per (event, division) pair and looks up the points via [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/lib/scoring/algorithms/index.ts#calculatePointsForPlace]], then pushes an `eventResult` with `formattedScore: "N/A"` and adds the points to `totalPoints`. Without this, missing-score totals would default to 0 and outrank scored athletes under "lowest total wins" algorithms like `online`. Divisions where no athlete has scored an event still fall through to the empty-results fallback (rank 0, 0 points, not added to totals).
128	
129	### Multi-round time caps
130	
131	For multi-round `time-with-cap` workouts, the cap is enforced **per round**, not against the summed total, and the summed total is preserved on the parent score rather than clamped to the cap.
132	
133	On video submissions, [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/video-submission-fns.ts#submitVideoFn]] compares each round's encoded time to `workout.timeCap * 1000`. Any round whose value meets or exceeds the cap is persisted with `scoreRoundsTable.status = "cap"`, and the parent `scoresTable.status` is set to `"cap"` whenever at least one round is capped. The parent `scoreValue` is the aggregation of rounds per the workout's `scoreType` (`sum` for "R1 + R2 = total" partner workouts; `min` for best-of). Under the "missed reps add seconds" convention a capped round's encoded value already includes its penalty, so the sum is meaningful. Single-round `time-with-cap` still clamps to the cap and records reps-at-cap in `secondaryValue`.
134

file.content:
The unified scoring system stores competition results in `scoresTable`, replacing a legacy results + sets approach.

Key design:
- Scores encode values as integers for efficient DB-level sorting
- `competitionEventId` links to the competition event (NULL for personal logs)
- `scheme` and `scoreType` determine how the raw value is interpreted
- Tiebreak values stored separately for time-capped workouts
- Score rounds (`scoreRoundsTable`) store per-round breakdowns for multi-round workouts

### One score per athlete per event per division

The `scoresTable` unique index spans `(competition_event_id, user_id, scaling_level_id)` so an athlete registered in multiple divisions can hold a separate score per division on a shared workout.

Column-name note: `scores.competition_event_id` is misleadingly named — it actually stores a `track_workouts.id` (the value the caller supplies as `trackWorkoutId`), not a `competition_events.id`. Every insert path assigns it from `data.trackWorkoutId`. That scope is exactly right for this invariant: when the same workout appears in multiple divisions via event-division mappings, both divisions reference the same `track_workout` row, so the unique key `(track_workout, user, scaling_level)` is what lets a multi-division athlete carry one score per division.

Online competitions allow the same track workout to appear in multiple divisions (e.g. scheduled for both Individual and Partner), and athletes can register in more than one division. Keying only on `(track_workout, user)` would merge an athlete's individual and partner scores into one row via `onDuplicateKeyUpdate` — the last submitted score would overwrite the other, and every leaderboard/athlete-detail read (which joins on `track_workout + user`) would surface that single row against every registration the user owned, leaking partner scores onto the individual leaderboard and vice versa. Every score-write path ([[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/video-submission-fns.ts#submitVideoFn]], [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/competition-score-fns.ts#saveCompetitionScoreFn]], [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/athlete-score-fns.ts#submitAthleteScoreFn]], [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/submission-verification-fns.ts]], [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/routes/api/compete/scores/submit.ts]], [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/routes/api/compete/scores/judge.ts]], [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/routes/api/compete/video/submit.ts]]) scopes the registration lookup by divisionId and carries that divisionId through to the upsert's `scalingLevelId`. Submission fns that accept an athlete without a divisionId reject ambiguously when the athlete is registered in more than one division rather than silently picking one. Every score-read path ([[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server/competition-leaderboard.ts#getCompetitionLeaderboard]], [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server/series-leaderboard.ts]], [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/organizer-athlete-fns.ts#getOrganizerAthleteDetailFn]], [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/division-results-fns.ts]]) scopes scores by `scaling_level_id` and groups/attributes by the score's own `scalingLevelId` rather than by the first registration found for that userId — the leaderboard maps each score to the exact `(user, division)` registration, not just userId. Delete paths ([[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/competition-score-fns.ts#deleteCompetitionScoreFn]], [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/registration-fns.ts#removeRegistrationFn]]) scope by division so removing one registration or score doesn't nuke the athlete's scores in their other division on a shared workout. The registration-transfer path in [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server/commerce/transfer-handlers.ts]] intentionally deletes all of the source user's scores across divisions because the user is being fully transferred out of the competition.

### Time input parsing matches save semantics

Bare numeric input on a time score (no colon — e.g. `2000`) is treated as **raw seconds** in both the live preview and the save path, so the two can never disagree.

[[apps/wodsmith-start/src/lib/scoring/parse/time.ts#parseTime]] defers to [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/lib/scoring/encode/time.ts#encodeTime]] for any non-colon input. An earlier version smart-padded bare digits as MM:SS (`2000` → `20:00` in preview) while the save persisted `33:20`, which confused athletes typing on a phone. Colon-delimited input (`12:34`, `1:02:34.567`) is unchanged. Use `precision: "ms"` only when explicitly persisting raw milliseconds.

### Missing scores tie at worst place

Athletes registered in a division who never submitted a score for an event are awarded the same points as if they finished one place behind every recorded score in their division.

If a division has 5 recorded scores for an event, every athlete in that division without a score ties at place 6 and receives the algorithm-appropriate points for place 6 (e.g. 6 points under online scoring, `firstPlacePoints − 5×step` under traditional). [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server/competition-leaderboard.ts#getCompetitionLeaderboard]] computes `missingPlace = activeCount + 1` per (event, division) pair and looks up the points via [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/lib/scoring/algorithms/index.ts#calculatePointsForPlace]], then pushes an `eventResult` with `formattedScore: "N/A"` and adds the points to `totalPoints`. Without this, missing-score totals would default to 0 and outrank scored athletes under "lowest total wins" algorithms like `online`. Divisions where no athlete has scored an event still fall through to the empty-results fallback (rank 0, 0 points, not added to totals).

### Multi-round time caps

For multi-round `time-with-cap` workouts, the cap is enforced **per round**, not against the summed total, and the summed total is preserved on the parent score rather than clamped to the cap.

On video submissions, [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/video-submission-fns.ts#submitVideoFn]] compares each round's encoded time to `workout.timeCap * 1000`. Any round whose value meets or exceeds the cap is persisted with `scoreRoundsTable.status = "cap"`, and the parent `scoresTable.status` is set to `"cap"` whenever at least one round is capped. The parent `scoreValue` is the aggregation of rounds per the workout's `scoreType` (`sum` for "R1 + R2 = total" partner workouts; `min` for best-of). Under the "missed reps add seconds" convention a capped round's encoded value already includes its penalty, so the sum is meaningful. Single-round `time-with-cap` still clamps to the cap and records reps-at-cap in `secondaryValue`.

input.args: /remember "When scoping score queries by `scalingLevelId`, treat null as its own scope (`isNull(scoresTable.scalingLevelId)`) — never drop the predicate. MySQL/PlanetScale UNIQUE indexes allow multiple NULLs, so a missing predicate falls back to (event, user) and leaks scores across divisions on shared workouts. Same pattern across athlete-score-fns, competition-score-fns, submission-verification-fns, video-submission-fns, organizer-athlete-fns, registration-fns, and the judge route. Also: `scores.competition_event_id` is misleadingly named — it stores a `track_workouts.id`, not a `competition_events.id`. Score deletes keyed off `competitionEventsTable.id` silently match nothing — use `competitionEventsTable.trackWorkoutId` instead." --category=gotcha --priority=critical

content.text:
Base directory for this skill: /Users/zacjones/Documents/02.Areas/wodsmith/thewodapp-2/.claude/skills/team-memory

# Team Memory

Manage a shared team memory system backed by a Cloudflare Worker with semantic search.

## Commands

### /remember — Store an observation

Save a new observation to team memory.

```bash
TEAM_MEMORY_URL=https://team-memory.zacjones93.workers.dev bun run .claude/skills/team-memory/scripts/remember.ts "<observation text>" [--category=<category>] [--priority=<priority>]
```

**Categories:** `convention`, `gotcha`, `debugging`, `architecture`, `workflow`
**Priorities:** `critical`, `moderate`, `ephemeral`

Defaults: category=convention, priority=moderate

Examples:
```bash
TEAM_MEMORY_URL=https://team-memory.zacjones93.workers.dev bun run .claude/skills/team-memory/scripts/remember.ts "PlanetScale returns strings for COUNT columns — always wrap with Number()" --category=gotcha --priority=critical
TEAM_MEMORY_URL=https://team-memory.zacjones93.workers.dev bun run .claude/skills/team-memory/scripts/remember.ts "Use ULID for ID generation, not CUID2" --category=convention
```

### /recall — Search memories

Search team memory by semantic query.

```bash
TEAM_MEMORY_URL=https://team-memory.zacjones93.workers.dev bun run .claude/skills/team-memory/scripts/recall.ts "<query>" [--limit=<n>] [--category=<category>] [--priority=<priority>]
```

Default limit: 5. Results are ranked by relevance.

Examples:
```bash
TEAM_MEMORY_URL=https://team-memory.zacjones93.workers.dev bun run .claude/skills/team-memory/scripts/recall.ts "database migration patterns"
TEAM_MEMORY_URL=https://team-memory.zacjones93.workers.dev bun run .claude/skills/team-memory/scripts/recall.ts "Stripe" --category=gotcha --limit=10
```

### /feedback — Rate a memory

Provide feedback on a memory's usefulness.

```bash
TEAM_MEMORY_URL=https://team-memory.zacjones93.workers.dev bun run .claude/skills/team-memory/scripts/feedback.ts <observation-id> <signal> [--note="<reason>"]
```

**Signals:** `helpful`, `harmful`, `irrelevant`

Examples:
```bash
TEAM_MEMORY_URL=https://team-memory.zacjones93.workers.dev bun run .claude/skills/team-memory/scripts/feedback.ts 01J5K3M2N7 helpful --note="Saved me from a production bug"
TEAM_MEMORY_URL=https://team-memory.zacjones93.workers.dev bun run .claude/skills/team-memory/scripts/feedback.ts 01J5K3M2N7 irrelevant
```

## Environment

Production URL: `https://team-memory.zacjones93.workers.dev`

All commands below include the env var inline. If `TEAM_MEMORY_URL` is already set in your shell, the prefix is optional.

## Hooks

- **SessionStart**: Reminds the agent about the memory system and how to use /recall for task-relevant lookups
- **SessionEnd**: Reads transcript JSONL, extracts user/assistant messages, POSTs to `/sessions`
- **Export**: Syncs memories to MEMORY.md between `<!-- BEGIN TEAM-MEMORY -->` / `<!-- END TEAM-MEMORY -->` markers


ARGUMENTS: /remember "When scoping score queries by `scalingLevelId`, treat null as its own scope (`isNull(scoresTable.scalingLevelId)`) — never drop the predicate. MySQL/PlanetScale UNIQUE indexes allow multiple NULLs, so a missing predicate falls back to (event, user) and leaks scores across divisions on shared workouts. Same pattern across athlete-score-fns, competition-score-fns, submission-verification-fns, video-submission-fns, organizer-athlete-fns, registration-fns, and the judge route. Also: `scores.competition_event_id` is misleadingly named — it stores a `track_workouts.id`, not a `competition_events.id`. Score deletes keyed off `competitionEventsTable.id` silently match nothing — use `competitionEventsTable.trackWorkoutId` instead." --category=gotcha --priority=critical

content.text:
Stored. Final summary:

## Addressed PR comments

**Null-division leak (recurring theme — applied `isNull` predicate everywhere)**
- `athlete-score-fns.ts` — refactored `resolveAthleteDivisionId` to discriminated union (`specific` | `open` | `ambiguous` | `notFound`); `getAthleteEventScoreFn` now returns empty score for ambiguous/notFound instead of leaking; post-upsert lookup uses `isNull` for null divisions
- `competition-score-fns.ts` — `deleteCompetitionScoreInputSchema.divisionId` now `nullable()` (required); existing-scores keyed by `(userId, scalingLevelId)`; post-upsert + delete always division-scoped
- `submission-verification-fns.ts` — all 3 condition builders (pre-tx, in-tx, post-insert) use `isNull` for null divisions
- `video-submission-fns.ts` — fixed score lookup, replaced `sql\`1=1\`` fallback with `isNull`, fixed organizer detail lookup
- `organizer-athlete-fns.ts` — both score-fetch and delete-score paths use `isNull`
- `routes/api/compete/scores/judge.ts` — post-upsert lookup uses `isNull`
- `event-score-card.tsx` — caller passes `null` to match new schema

**registration-fns score-deletion bug (cubic P1, valid)**
- Was selecting `competition_events.id` and comparing to `scores.competition_event_id` (which actually stores `track_workouts.id`) → deletes silently matched nothing. Fixed by selecting `competitionEventsTable.trackWorkoutId`.

**Docs**
- `lat.md/domain.md` wording nit ("reject ambiguously" → "reject with an ambiguity error"; "all of the" → "all the")

## Skipped (architectural — flagged for follow-up)
- Coderabbit/Cubic flagged that `scores` UNIQUE index on `(competition_event_id, user_id, scaling_level_id)` doesn't enforce uniqueness when `scaling_level_id` is NULL (MySQL allows multiple NULLs). The read-path `isNull` fixes prevent leaks, but real concurrent-insert duplicates remain possible for null-division submissions. Proper fix is a generated `COALESCE(scaling_level_id, '__open__')` column in the unique key — wanted to confirm with you before reshaping the schema.

`lat check` passes; type-check has only 2 pre-existing errors in `email.tsx` (unrelated to this PR).

file.content:
### One score per athlete per event per division

The `scoresTable` unique index spans `(competition_event_id, user_id, scaling_level_id)` so an athlete registered in multiple divisions can hold a separate score per division on a shared workout.

Column-name note: `scores.competition_event_id` is misleadingly named — it actually stores a `track_workouts.id` (the value the caller supplies as `trackWorkoutId`), not a `competition_events.id`. Every insert path assigns it from `data.trackWorkoutId`. That scope is exactly right for this invariant: when the same workout appears in multiple divisions via event-division mappings, both divisions reference the same `track_workout` row, so the unique key `(track_workout, user, scaling_level)` is what lets a multi-division athlete carry one score per division.

Online competitions allow the same track workout to appear in multiple divisions (e.g. scheduled for both Individual and Partner), and athletes can register in more than one division. Keying only on `(track_workout, user)` would merge an athlete's individual and partner scores into one row via `onDuplicateKeyUpdate` — the last submitted score would overwrite the other, and every leaderboard/athlete-detail read (which joins on `track_workout + user`) would surface that single row against every registration the user owned, leaking partner scores onto the individual leaderboard and vice versa. Every score-write path ([[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/video-submission-fns.ts#submitVideoFn]], [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/competition-score-fns.ts#saveCompetitionScoreFn]], [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/athlete-score-fns.ts#submitAthleteScoreFn]], [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/submission-verification-fns.ts]], [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/routes/api/compete/scores/submit.ts]], [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/routes/api/compete/scores/judge.ts]], [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/routes/api/compete/video/submit.ts]]) scopes the registration lookup by divisionId and carries that divisionId through to the upsert's `scalingLevelId`. Submission fns that accept an athlete without a divisionId reject with an ambiguity error when the athlete is registered in more than one division rather than silently picking one. Every score-read path ([[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server/competition-leaderboard.ts#getCompetitionLeaderboard]], [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server/series-leaderboard.ts]], [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/organizer-athlete-fns.ts#getOrganizerAthleteDetailFn]], [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/division-results-fns.ts]]) scopes scores by `scaling_level_id` and groups/attributes by the score's own `scalingLevelId` rather than by the first registration found for that userId — the leaderboard maps each score to the exact `(user, division)` registration, not just userId. Delete paths ([[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/competition-score-fns.ts#deleteCompetitionScoreFn]], [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/registration-fns.ts#removeRegistrationFn]]) scope by division so removing one registration or score doesn't nuke the athlete's scores in their other division on a shared workout. The registration-transfer path in [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server/commerce/transfer-handlers.ts]] intentionally deletes all the source user's scores across divisions because the user is being fully transferred out of the competition.

### Time input parsing matches save semantics

input.new_string:
### One score per athlete per event per division

The `scoresTable` unique index spans `(competition_event_id, user_id, scaling_level_id)` so an athlete registered in multiple divisions can hold a separate score per division on a shared workout.

Column-name note: `scores.competition_event_id` is misleadingly named — it actually stores a `track_workouts.id` (the value the caller supplies as `trackWorkoutId`), not a `competition_events.id`. Every insert path assigns it from `data.trackWorkoutId`. That scope is exactly right for this invariant: when the same workout appears in multiple divisions via event-division mappings, both divisions reference the same `track_workout` row, so the unique key `(track_workout, user, scaling_level)` is what lets a multi-division athlete carry one score per division. The same naming gotcha bites delete paths: keying a score delete off `competitionEventsTable.id` matches nothing because that column holds the competition-event row id, not a `track_workouts.id` — score deletes must select `competitionEventsTable.trackWorkoutId` and compare to `scoresTable.competitionEventId`.

Online competitions allow the same track workout to appear in multiple divisions (e.g. scheduled for both Individual and Partner), and athletes can register in more than one division. Keying only on `(track_workout, user)` would merge an athlete's individual and partner scores into one row via `onDuplicateKeyUpdate` — the last submitted score would overwrite the other, and every leaderboard/athlete-detail read (which joins on `track_workout + user`) would surface that single row against every registration the user owned, leaking partner scores onto the individual leaderboard and vice versa. Every score-write path ([[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/video-submission-fns.ts#submitVideoFn]], [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/competition-score-fns.ts#saveCompetitionScoreFn]], [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/athlete-score-fns.ts#submitAthleteScoreFn]], [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/submission-verification-fns.ts]], [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/routes/api/compete/scores/submit.ts]], [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/routes/api/compete/scores/judge.ts]], [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/routes/api/compete/video/submit.ts]]) scopes the registration lookup by divisionId and carries that divisionId through to the upsert's `scalingLevelId`. Submission fns that accept an athlete without a divisionId reject with an ambiguity error when the athlete is registered in more than one division rather than silently picking one. Every score-read path ([[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server/competition-leaderboard.ts#getCompetitionLeaderboard]], [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server/series-leaderboard.ts]], [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/organizer-athlete-fns.ts#getOrganizerAthleteDetailFn]], [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/division-results-fns.ts]]) scopes scores by `scaling_level_id` and groups/attributes by the score's own `scalingLevelId` rather than by the first registration found for that userId — the leaderboard maps each score to the exact `(user, division)` registration, not just userId. Delete paths ([[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/competition-score-fns.ts#deleteCompetitionScoreFn]], [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/registration-fns.ts#removeRegistrationFn]]) scope by division so removing one registration or score doesn't nuke the athlete's scores in their other division on a shared workout. The registration-transfer path in [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server/commerce/transfer-handlers.ts]] intentionally deletes all the source user's scores across divisions because the user is being fully transferred out of the competition.

#### Null divisionId is its own scope

When `scalingLevelId` is null on a registration (open / no-division), score read/write/delete predicates use `isNull(scoresTable.scalingLevelId)` — never drop the predicate. MySQL/PlanetScale UNIQUE indexes treat NULL as distinct, so a missing `scalingLevelId` predicate falls back to `(event, user)` and pulls in every other division's score for the same shared workout. Pattern recurs in every score path; `divisionId ? eq(scalingLevelId, divisionId) : isNull(scalingLevelId)` is the canonical predicate.

[[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/competition-score-fns.ts#deleteCompetitionScoreFn]] takes `divisionId: z.string().nullable()` — required-but-nullable — so callers must explicitly pick a scope. Optional was unsafe: a missing field collapsed to an unscoped delete that wiped every division's score for the user/workout pair. [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/athlete-score-fns.ts#getAthleteEventScoreFn]] uses `resolveAthleteDivisionId`, which returns a `{ kind: "specific" | "open" | "ambiguous" | "notFound" }` discriminated union; ambiguous and not-found cases short-circuit to an empty score rather than dropping the divisionId predicate (which would leak across divisions). [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/competition-score-fns.ts#getEventScoreEntryDataFn]] keys its score map by `(userId, scalingLevelId)` rather than `userId` alone, so a multi-division athlete's division-A score never gets attached to their division-B registration row.

The registration-transfer path in [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server/commerce/transfer-handlers.ts]] intentionally deletes all the source user's scores across divisions because the user is being fully transferred out of the competition.
+#### Null divisionId is its own scope

+When `scalingLevelId` is null on a registration (open / no-division), score read/write/delete predicates use `isNull(scoresTable.scalingLevelId)` — never drop the predicate. MySQL/PlanetScale UNIQUE indexes treat NULL as distinct, so a missing `scalingLevelId` predicate falls back to `(event, user)` and pulls in every other division's score for the same shared workout. Pattern recurs in every score path; `divisionId ? eq(scalingLevelId, divisionId) : isNull(scalingLevelId)` is the canonical predicate.

+[[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/competition-score-fns.ts#deleteCompetitionScoreFn]] takes `divisionId: z.string().nullable()` — required-but-nullable — so callers must explicitly pick a scope. Optional was unsafe: a missing field collapsed to an unscoped delete that wiped every division's score for the user/workout pair. [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/athlete-score-fns.ts#getAthleteEventScoreFn]] uses `resolveAthleteDivisionId`, which returns a `{ kind: "specific" | "open" | "ambiguous" | "notFound" }` discriminated union; ambiguous and not-found cases short-circuit to an empty score rather than dropping the divisionId predicate (which would leak across divisions). [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/competition-score-fns.ts#getEventScoreEntryDataFn]] keys its score map by `(userId, scalingLevelId)` rather than `userId` alone, so a multi-division athlete's division-A score never gets attached to their division-B registration row.
### Time input parsing matches save semantics
Bare numeric input on a time score (no colon — e.g. `2000`) is treated as **raw seconds** in both the live preview and the save path, so the two can never disagree.

input.command:
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
fix(scoring): treat null divisionId as its own scope across score paths

Address PR review feedback: score read/write/delete paths previously dropped
the scalingLevelId predicate when divisionId was null, falling back to
(event, user) and leaking scores across divisions on shared workouts (MySQL
treats multiple NULLs as distinct in unique indexes).

- Canonical predicate `divisionId ? eq : isNull` applied across athlete-score,
  competition-score, submission-verification, video-submission,
  organizer-athlete fns and the judge route
- `deleteCompetitionScoreInputSchema.divisionId` is now `nullable()` (required)
  so callers must pick a scope; optional collapsed to unscoped delete
- `resolveAthleteDivisionId` returns a discriminated union; ambiguous and
  not-found resolutions short-circuit to an empty score instead of leaking
- `getEventScoreEntryDataFn` keys score map by `(userId, scalingLevelId)` so
  a multi-div athlete's division-A score never attaches to division-B reg
- `removeRegistrationFn` was selecting `competition_events.id` and comparing
  to `scores.competition_event_id` (which actually stores `track_workouts.id`)
  — score deletes silently matched nothing; now selects `trackWorkoutId`

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)" 2>&1 | tail -30

content.text:
Found the failure. Single test:

`test/server-fns/competition-score-fns.test.ts:310` — `getEventScoreEntryDataFn > returns athletes with existing scores` — expects `existingResult.resultId === 'score-1'` but got `undefined`.

This is from my change keying scores by `(userId, scalingLevelId)` instead of `userId` alone. The test fixture probably uses a score with no `scalingLevelId` (legacy) or a mismatched divisionId.

content.text:
Pushed `dcde721f`.

## Failing test diagnosed & fixed

Single test failed across both check runs:

**`test/server-fns/competition-score-fns.test.ts:310` — `getEventScoreEntryDataFn > returns athletes with existing scores`**
- `expected undefined to be 'score-1'`
- Root cause: my refactor in `getEventScoreEntryDataFn` keys the scores map by `(userId, scalingLevelId)` — required to stop a multi-division athlete's division-A score from getting attached to their division-B registration row. The test fixture's score row didn't have `scalingLevelId`, so lookup key `user-1::div-1` (from registration) didn't match `user-1::` (from score).
- Fix: added `scalingLevelId: 'div-1'` to the existing-score fixture so the score logically belongs to the registration's division.

All 10 tests in the file now pass locally. Pushed; CI will re-run.
